title: Dependency Vulnerability Alerts

compliance.assurance_rationale: ASD Essential Eight E8.06 Patch operating systems — dependency-vulnerability alerting is the primary operational evidence for patching software dependencies under the E8 patching strategy.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ASD E8 E8.06 (Patch operating systems) is enforced — Splunk UC-12.3.2: Dependency Vulnerability Alerts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-12.3.2' running on SCA tool output (Snyk, Dependabot, GitHub Advisory), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: SCA tool output (Snyk, Dependabot, GitHub Advisory)
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="snyk:vulnerability"
| where severity IN ("critical","high")
| stats count by project, package_name, cve_id, severity
| sort -severity, -count
description: Vulnerable dependencies in the software supply chain are a primary attack vector. Tracking ensures timely patching.
value: Vulnerable dependencies in the software supply chain are a primary attack vector. Tracking ensures timely patching.
implementation: Ingest SCA scan results. Track vulnerable dependencies by project, severity, and package. Alert on new critical/high findings. Track remediation time. Report on dependency health per team.
visualization: Table (vulnerable dependencies), Bar chart (vulns by project), Line chart (vulnerability trend), Single value (critical vulns count).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Snyk/Dependabot webhook.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: SCA tool output (Snyk, Dependabot, GitHub Advisory).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest SCA scan results. Track vulnerable dependencies by project, severity, and package. Alert on new critical/high findings. Track remediation time. Report on dependency health per team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="snyk:vulnerability"
| where severity IN ("critical","high")
| stats count by project, package_name, cve_id, severity
| sort -severity, -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Dependency Vulnerability Alerts** — Vulnerable dependencies in the software supply chain are a primary attack vector. Tracking ensures timely patching.

Documented **Data sources**: SCA tool output (Snyk, Dependabot, GitHub Advisory). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Snyk/Dependabot webhook. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: snyk:vulnerability. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="snyk:vulnerability". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("critical","high")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by project, package_name, cve_id, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GitHub audit log. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (vulnerable dependencies), Bar chart (vulns by project), Line chart (vulnerability trend), Single value (critical vulns count).
grandmaExplanation: We watch this part of your delivery chain for risks and reliability so issues show up before they hit customers.
knownFalsePositives: Alerts from scheduled scans, pilot repositories, or vendor API throttling during bulk operations.
